title: Lachryma
review: Lachryma which means 'tear' in Latin is the perfect example of Ghosts ability to write a powerful yet catchy rock/metal track. The song starts off with a synth melody, which ghost fans will notice uses the same melody and progression of 2015's Hit song 'Square Hammer'. The song then kicks into a super powerful and punchy guitar riff that screams metal. The chorus of this song features a beautiful melody and is very reminiscent of an 80's power-ballad, it makes you want to scream-sing along! Lyrically I think this song is very strong, with lyrics referring to an entity of sorts 'in the middle of the night it feeds, in the middle of the night it eats you!', and the chorus 'crying, over someone like you. crying, hope you're feeling it too...'. I believe that the entity in this song is not that of a person, but that of an addiction or mental illness, however it is up to interpretation. Even though this is my interpretation, this song could be classified as a break-up song describing an ex lover. I would highly recommend this song to anybody willing to step into the realm of Ghost, it is in my opinion one of their best works!

title: Made Her Mine
review: Exploding out of no-where, Dogma is an all female metal concept band that released their debut (their only album) in 2024. A lot of their music is very graphic in nature with highly sexual themes, including their super controversial music videos, however i wont be diving into all of that. I believe their song 'Made Her Mine' is their strongest song to date. This song uses a variety of instruments ranging from traditional metal percussion and strings, while mixing in some traditional strings in their to make the song sound that much more epic! Musically this song is very rich, killer riff to open the track which evolves into this super chuggy riff after the intro concludes. The chorus of this song is a bit less metal focused and transforms into a more pop/rock catchy-ness that you wont get out of your head. Add in a killer guitar and synth solo and you got one track that you cant stop banging your head too. Lyrically this song is about a nun or religious figure who struggles with desires that aren't seen as morally correct, in the eyes of their lord. As the song name implies, lyrically this song also features a second nun who gets roped into this entire plot, and without getting into too much detail the narrator of this story 'Made Her Mine'. Bonus points because the Lady who recorded the vocals for this track sounds like a metal Lady Gaga! (listen to this one at your own risk, there is super strong graphic themes!)

title: Beast and the Harlot
review: Beast and the Harlot by Avenged Sevenfold may be musically one of the best songs I have ever heard! A7X's musical prowess and their status as musical gods maybe overlooked because of their metal-core roots or even current releases that may not be the best in the eyes of the masses, however all it takes is a trip back to 2005 for me to remember who they really are. Musically this song features one of the coolest main riffs I have ever heard, lead guitarist Synyster Gates is even quoted saying that the riff was super difficult for him to perform live at first, and that he had to practice it over and over to get it right. The chorus of this song features an ascending melody that is just the right amount of bright and bubbly. The coolest parts for me are the two solo's. Synyster Gates guitar solo in this song is my favorite of all time! Just like the chorus, this solo is very bright sounding, with Syns technical ability fully on display. After Syns beautiful shredding, later in the song we get a powerful drum solo from the late great Jimmy 'The Rev' Sullivan, to this day I still do not know how his feet move so quickly, he has played this part live (flawlessly I may add) so its not a matter of production tricks! Lyrically this song is about the fall of Babylon, a historical even that happened in 539 BCE which i know nothing about, however this song puts a nice modern twist on the ancient tale!
